sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA big-picture strip (multi-table mode / sub-table filter): the strip now correctly updates when the sub-table filter (STF — the per-sub-table search input below the h3 header) is used. Previously the STF hid rows via CSS (display:none / data-mb-stf-hidden) without touching the bigbox, so the strip continued to show artwork for all rows regardless of the filter. Fix: two new helpers, caaUpdateBigBoxForTable(table, tableIndex) and eaaUpdateBigBoxForTable(table, tableIndex), are called at the end of every applySubFilter pass and clearSubFilter pass. Each helper (1) collects hrefs of release/release-group (CAA) or event (EAA) anchors in currently-visible rows; (2) shows/hides the corresponding bigbox wrapper elements; (3) updates the toggle-button badge to reflect the count of images that are both shown in the strip and already downloaded.

CAA big-picture strip (single-table mode): the strip was missing for page types that have features.addCAA configured but no caa column extractor, specifically series-releases (series/Releases tab, addCAA: 'Release') and artist-recordings (artist/Recordings tab, addCAA: 'Release groups'). The root cause was that initCaaPics() required a 'CAA' column header before building the bigbox; pages without the caa extractor never produce that header so the entire big-pic path was skipped. Fix: the table-level guard in initCaaPics() now accepts a table when EITHER a 'CAA' column header exists OR the active page definition carries features.addCAA. caaInitSmallPics() (which loads artwork into CAA-column icon spans) remains gated on hasCaaColumn only. An additional early-return is added when caaCountLinks() finds zero release/release-group links so tables that pass the relaxed guard but genuinely have no artwork links are still skipped without building an empty bigbox.
